title: 10 best herbal essential oils for indigestion
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for indigestion are natural remedies derived from plants that help alleviate symptoms associated with indigestion, such as bloating, nausea, and abdominal pain. The best herbal essential oils for indigestion include Zingiber officinale, Foeniculum vulgare, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Mentha x piperita, Cinnamomum verum, Curcuma longa, Cinchona officinalis, Cuminum cyminum, Carum carvi, and Anethum graveolens. If left untreated, indigestion can lead to chronic discomfort, fatigue, and decreased productivity, affecting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with indigestion because it contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol that have natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues such as bloating, nausea, and stomach discomfort. When inhaled or applied topically, the oil can stimulate digestion and alleviate symptoms of indigestion by reducing inflammation in the stomach lining and improving gut motility, making it a natural and effective remedy for promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Herbal ginger essential oil's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of indigestion and bloating.

Digestive Enzyme Stimulant Properties: The oils' volatile compounds stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, enhancing digestion and absorption of nutrients, which can help alleviate nausea, bloating, and discomfort associated with indigestion.

Spasmolytic and Carminative Properties: Herbal ginger essential oil's ability to relax smooth muscle tissues and its carminative effects (preventing gas and bloating) can help soothe spasms in the digestive tract, reducing symptoms of indigestion such as cramps, flatulence, and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are good for making medicinal essential oils for indigestion because they contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the thick, underground stems of the ginger plant that are rich in volatile oils, making them an excellent source for extracting essential oils with digestive benefits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger roots weighing about 100 grams cut them into small pieces.
Dry the ginger pieces in a warm place or using a food dehydrator for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried ginger pieces using 500 milliliters of water at atmospheric pressure.
Collect and filter the extracted essential oil through a coffee filter paper within 30 minutes.
Store the obtained zingiber officinale essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with indigestion because of its natural digestive properties. The menthol content in peppermint oil helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause discomfort. Additionally, peppermint oil's calming effect on the stomach lining also reduces inflammation and irritation, allowing for smoother digestion and alleviating symptoms such as bloating, gas, and nausea. By using herbal peppermint essential oils, individuals can experience relief from indigestion and promote a healthy digestive system.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Peppermint essential oil helps relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps associated with indigestion.
Carminative property: The oil's natural compounds help to release trapped gas and reduce bloating, which can alleviate symptoms of indigestion such as discomfort and pressure.
Digestive stimulant property: Peppermint essential oil can increase digestive enzymes and improve the absorption of nutrients, helping to restore normal digestion and alleviate indigestion symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of peppermint contain a high concentration of menthol, which is responsible for its digestive properties and helps to relieve indigestion.
Leaves: Peppermint leaves are rich in menthone, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and can help to soothe digestive issues such as indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves at dawn when dew is present to ensure maximum oil content.
Dry the harvested peppermint leaves in a warm dark place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture.
Blend 500g of dried peppermint leaves with 1l of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a glass jar.
Steam distill the blend at 100°c for 2 hours using a stainless steel still and collect the resulting essential oil.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter to remove impurities and store it in a dark glass bottle for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with indigestion because of its natural carminative properties, which aid in relieving bloating and gas. The oil's active compounds, such as anethole and fenchone, work together to calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and spasms that can contribute to discomfort. Additionally, fennel essential oils stimulate digestion and improve nutrient absorption, allowing the body to properly process food and reduce symptoms of indigestion, leading to a more comfortable and balanced digestive system.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Fennel essential oil has a carminative property, which means it helps to relieve flatulence and bloating by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract and reducing spasms.

Digestive stimulant: Fennel essential oil stimulates digestion and improves peristalsis (the movement of food through the digestive system), helping to reduce symptoms of indigestion such as cramps, bloating, and discomfort.
Antispasmodic: Fennel essential oil has an antispasmodic property, which helps to relieve spasms in the digestive tract and alleviate cramps, reducing the severity of indigestion symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the fennel plant contain compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation and relax the digestive system, making them effective in alleviating indigestion symptoms.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain essential fatty acids and phytochemicals that have natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help soothe digestive issues such as indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare seeds when mature and dry them thoroughly in a warm oven or by spreading them out in the sun.
Crush the dried fennel seeds into small pieces using a mortar and pestle to release their aromatic oils.
Steam distill the crushed fennel seed mixture in a still with water for 3-4 hours to extract the essential oil.
Collect the steam-distilled essential oil in a separate container, allowing it to settle and clarify before use.
Store the extracted foeniculum vulgare essential oil in dark glass bottles away from direct sunlight and heat.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with indigestion because of its natural calming properties that soothe the digestive tract. The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce irritation and discomfort, while its gentle antispasmodic effects ease cramps and spasms in the stomach and intestines. Additionally, chamomile's ability to relax the muscles in the digestive tract allows for smoother digestion and reduced symptoms of indigestion such as bloating, gas, and discomfort. This natural remedy promotes a sense of calm and relaxation, making it an effective way to alleviate indigestion symptoms without harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help soothe irritated digestive tissues, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with indigestion.
Anxiolytic: Chamomile oil has a calming effect on the mind and body, which can help alleviate anxiety-related digestive issues such as indigestion, cramps, and diarrhea.
Antispasmodic: Chamomile oil's antispasmodic properties can relax muscle spasms in the digestive tract, providing relief from cramps, bloating, and discomfort caused by indigestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin and luteolin, chamomile roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe digestive issues and reduce symptoms of indigestion.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers contain a high concentration of azulene compounds, which are known for their calming effects on the digestive system and can help alleviate stomach discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and leaves at dawn for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 12 hours or air-drying for 24 hours.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for efficient steam distillation.
Steam distill the chopped plant material in a still using 50% water and 50% vapor ratio at atmospheric pressure for 2-3 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through coffee filters or cotton gauze to remove impurities and store it in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with indigestion because of its natural soothing properties, which can alleviate symptoms of digestive discomfort. The oil's calming effects on the stomach muscles help to reduce spasms and cramps, allowing for a smoother digestion process. Additionally, lemon balm essential oils have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and irritation in the digestive tract, further relieving indigestion symptoms. By incorporating this natural remedy into your daily routine, you may experience significant relief from discomfort and bloating associated with indigestion.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Lemon balm essential oil helps to relax the smooth muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps associated with indigestion.
Carminative property: Lemon balm essential oil has the ability to release trapped gas in the stomach and intestines, reducing bloating and flatulence.
Soothing and anti-inflammatory property: Lemon balm essential oil has a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to indigestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are good for making medicinal essential oils for indigestion because they contain a high concentration of camphor, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate digestive discomfort.
Leaves: Lemon balm leaves are used to create essential oils that soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing symptoms of indigestion such as bloating and cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and stems ensuring minimal bruising or damage.
Dry the plant material using air circulation at room temperature for two days.
Steam distill 100g of dried melissa officinalis with 500ml of distilled water for 30 minutes.
Collect and filter the essential oil through a coffee filter paper into a dark glass bottle.
Store the final product in an airtight container away from direct sunlight for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree essential oils helps with indigestion because of its natural carminative properties, which aid in relieving bloating and discomfort. The oil's compounds, such as ginkgolides and bilobalide, have been shown to soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and spasms that can lead to indigestion. Additionally, maidenhair tree essential oils has a calming effect on the stomach, helping to alleviate nausea and discomfort associated with indigestion. Its use promotes healthy digestion and relieves symptoms of indigestion, making it a natural and effective remedy.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba essential oil has antioxidant properties that can help protect the gut lining from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to indigestion.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The essential oil of Ginkgo biloba has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of indigestion such as bloating and discomfort.

Digestive relaxant properties: Some studies suggest that Ginkgo biloba extracts may have a relaxing effect on the smooth muscle of the digestive tract, which can help alleviate cramps and spasms associated with indigestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds like sesquiterpenes and diterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate digestive issues.

Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, the barks of maidenhair trees have been used traditionally to treat indigestion and other gastrointestinal disorders due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves measure 20 grams and wash them thoroughly with distilled water.
Steam distill the harvested ginkgo biloba leaves at a temperature of 100°c for 3 hours to extract the oil.
Collect and separate the obtained essential oil from the water and impurities using a separation funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a sterile cotton cloth or paper filter to remove any remaining particles or sediment.
Transfer the filtered ginkgo biloba essential oil to dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric essential oils helps with indigestion because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation caused by irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) or other digestive disorders. The oil also has carminative properties, helping to expel gas from the body, alleviating bloating and discomfort. Additionally, turmeric's curcuminoids have been shown to improve digestion, stimulate bile production, and enhance nutrient absorption, making it a natural and effective remedy for indigestion relief.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Turmeric essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of indigestion such as bloating and discomfort.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidant properties of turmeric essential oil help to protect the digestive system from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to indigestion and other digestive issues.

Carminative property: Turmeric essential oil's carminative properties help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce spasms, which can alleviate symptoms of indigestion such as cramping and bloating.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Turmeric rhyzomes contain high amounts of curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation associated with indigestion.

Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant are rich in bioactive compounds like curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin, making them a valuable source for creating medicinal essential oils that aid digestion and alleviate symptoms of indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh turmeric roots weighing about one kilogram.
Dry the turmeric roots in shade at room temperature for five days.
Blend two hundred grams of dried turmeric powder with one liter of carrier oil.
Steam distill the mixture under vacuum pressure at 100 degrees celsius for four hours.
Filter and decant the essential oil after cooling it to twenty degrees celsius.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dill essential oils helps with indigestion because of its natural carminative properties. The oil's volatile compounds, such as dillenol and dillapiole, can help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to indigestion. Additionally, dill oil has been shown to improve digestion by stimulating the release of enzymes that break down food, making it easier for the body to absorb nutrients. As a result, dill essential oils can help to soothe and calm indigestion symptoms, providing relief from discomfort and promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Digestive stimulant: Dill essential oil stimulates digestion, helping to break down food more efficiently and relieve symptoms of indigestion such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort.
Carminative: Dill essential oil has carminative properties, which means it can help expel gas and reduce bloating by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract and reducing inflammation.

Antispasmodic: Dill essential oil has antispasmodic properties, which can help relieve spasms and cramps in the digestive tract, providing relief from indigestion-related discomforts such as stomach pain and nausea.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that help to soothe digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of indigestion.
Leaves: Dill leaves are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20 grams of dried anethum graveolens leaves and flowers from a reputable supplier or your own harvest.
Use a mortar and pestle to grind the dried herbs into a fine powder for 5 minutes, releasing their aromatic oils.
Combine the powdered herb with 500 milliliters of high-proof ethanol in a glass jar, covering it with a lid to prevent evaporation.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction and infusion.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another container, discarding the solids and preserving the clear essential oil extract for further use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Coriander essential oils helps with indigestion because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive system. It also contains antioxidants that reduce oxidative stress in the gut, alleviating symptoms of indigestion such as bloating and discomfort. Additionally, coriander's calming effects on the stomach muscles help to relax and ease digestion, making it an effective natural remedy for promoting healthy digestion and reducing indigestion symptoms.
remedy_properties: Carminative property: Herbal coriander essential oil helps to expel gas from the digestive system, which can alleviate symptoms of bloating and discomfort associated with indigestion.

Anti-inflammatory property: Coriander oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining and intestines, which can contribute to indigestion and other digestive issues.
Spasmolytic property: Coriander oil's spasmolytic properties can help relax the smooth muscle in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can occur due to indigestion and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Coriander roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing digestive issues.
Seeds: Coriander seeds contain a compound called linalool, which helps to calm digestive problems such as nausea and bloating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh coriandrum sativum leaves and flowers in the morning for optimal oil yield and flavor.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at 30 degrees celsius for eight hours.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a stainless steel still with one kilogram of plant material per liter of distilled water for two hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter paper and transfer to dark glass bottles for storage at room temperature.
Store the coriandrum sativum essential oil in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight and heat sources for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with indigestion because of its natural antispasmodic properties, which help to soothe and calm the digestive tract. When ingested or inhaled, sage oil can ease symptoms of indigestion such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort by relaxing the muscles and reducing inflammation. Additionally, sage's antimicrobial properties may help to eliminate harmful bacteria that can contribute to indigestion, promoting a healthy gut environment and supporting overall digestive well-being.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Sage essential oil helps to relieve indigestion by reducing gas and bloating, allowing for easier digestion and alleviating discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory: Sage oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and calm irritated stomach tissues, reducing inflammation and pain associated with indigestion.
Antispasmodic: Sage essential oil helps to relax the muscles of the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps caused by indigestion, allowing for smoother digestion and relief from discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots are valued for their ability to calm digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the stomach, making them a popular choice for treating indigestion.
Barks: The barks of the sage plant contain compounds that have natural antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate cramps and spasms associated with indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves in sufficient quantities for the desired yield of essential oil.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 12 hours to preserve their potency.
Steam distill the dried leaves in a still using a 10:1 ratio of water to plant material for 3 hours to extract the oils.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separating funnel and store it in a dark glass bottle with a tight seal.
Allow the essential oil to mature for at least 24 hours before use to allow its components to stabilize and blend.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat indigestion with herbal essential oils is to blend a few drops of peppermint, ginger, and chamomile oils with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Apply the blend topically to the abdomen after meals to relieve discomfort and cramps. Alternatively, inhale the vapors of these oils using a diffuser to soothe digestive issues.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to indigestion that are treated with herbal essential oils are bloating, cramps, and flatulence. Ginger essential oil is often used to alleviate these symptoms due to its anti-inflammatory properties. Peppermint oil is also effective in soothing digestive issues by calming the stomach muscles and reducing spasms. Additionally, fennel oil can help relieve gas and bloating by promoting digestion and relieving discomfort. These herbal essential oils can be used topically or added to a warm bath for relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with indigestion because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and digestive properties. Gingerols and shogaols, the active components in ginger, help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of indigestion, such as bloating and nausea. By soothing the stomach lining and stimulating digestion, ginger essential oil can ease discomfort and promote a sense of calm, making it a popular natural remedy for digestive issues.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive discomfort associated with indigestion.
Shogaols: Shogaols exhibit anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic effects, which help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce spasms that can contribute to indigestion.
Zingiberene: As a terpene, zingiberene has anti-inflammatory and carminative properties, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate gas and bloating associated with indigestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Utilized for essential oils to treat indigestion because they contain bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols that aid in digestion and relieve nausea.
Stems: Employed for essential oils to ease indigestion symptoms as they contain volatile oils that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 3-5 kg of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes from mature plants in late summer or early autumn.
Steam distill the rhizomes at a temperature of 100-150°c and pressure of 2-3 bar for 3-4 hours.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the hydrosol through decantation or centrifugation.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 μm membrane filter to remove any impurities or contaminants.
Store the pure essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare essential oils helps with indigestion because of its carminative properties, which help to alleviate digestive discomfort and relieve gas. The oil's anethole content has been shown to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can lead to indigestion. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties may help to soothe an irritated digestive system, making it a popular natural remedy for digestive issues. Its calming effects on the digestive system can provide relief from indigestion and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Bergamotene: This terpene constituent has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of indigestion.
Selinene: This sesquiterpene constituent has carminative properties, which help to relieve gas and bloating associated with indigestion.
Anethole: This phenolic ether constituent has spasmolytic properties, which help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of indigestion such as cramping and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used for their carminative and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to ease digestive discomfort.
Leaves: The leaves contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation and improve digestion, making them a popular choice for essential oil production.
Buds: The buds are rich in volatile oils that have carminative and antispasmodic properties, helping to alleviate indigestion and other digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh foeniculum vulgare flowers and leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested flowers and leaves in a single layer on a paper bag in a warm dark place for 2 days.
Steam distill the dried flowers and leaves at 100-110 degrees celsius for 3-4 hours to extract the essential oil.
Filter the extracted essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any remaining plant particles and debris.
Store the final foeniculum vulgare essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight-fitting lid in a cool place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils helps with indigestion because of its potent anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The licorice root extracts present in the oil have been traditionally used to calm digestive issues, including heartburn and bloating. By reducing inflammation in the stomach lining, Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils can provide relief from discomfort and alleviate digestive symptoms. Additionally, its antispasmodic properties can help to regulate bowel movements, promoting a healthy and balanced digestive system. This results in improved digestion and reduced symptoms of indigestion.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: They help with indigestion by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and protecting the stomach lining from acid damage.
Flavonoids: They help with indigestion by reducing oxidative stress and improving gut health, which can help alleviate symptoms of indigestion such as bloating and discomfort.
Coumarins: They help with indigestion by acting as a natural antispasmodic, relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract and reducing cramps and spasms that can contribute to indigestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Glycyrrhizin-rich root extracts are used to make essential oils for indigestion as they contain compounds that aid in soothing digestive issues.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that contribute to their use in essential oils for indigestion relief.
Roots: The rhizome of Glycyrrhiza glabra, which is often referred to as roots, is also used for its medicinal properties, including aiding in digestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in late summer or early fall when the plant is dormant.
Steam distill the harvested roots for 4 hours to extract the essential oil using a 1:5 ratio of plant material to water.
Collect and separate the essential oil from the distillate using a separating funnel and store it in a dark glass bottle.
Filter the collected essential oil through a 10-micron filter paper to remove any impurities and improve its quality.
Store the final essential oil in a cool dark place away from direct sunlight for up to 12 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oils helps with indigestion because its active compounds, such as menthol and menthone, have natural anti-inflammatory and carminative properties. These properties help to ease digestive discomfort, reduce nausea and bloating, and relax the muscles in the digestive tract, promoting smoother digestion and reducing symptoms of indigestion. remedy_constituents: Menthone: Helps with indigestion by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviating symptoms of nausea and vomiting.
Menthyl acetate: Eases indigestion by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract and reducing spasms that can cause discomfort.
Limonene: Assists in reducing indigestion by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause digestive issues.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of peppermint contain high amounts of menthol and menthone, which are responsible for its digestive benefits.
Stems: The stems of peppermint contain a higher concentration of menthone, a compound that helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and alleviate indigestion.
Roots: The roots of peppermint have a higher concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mentha x piperita leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated and before heat of day.
Steam distill 20 kg of fresh mentha x piperita leaves with 20 liters of distilled water for 2 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distillate by decanting it into a glass container and storing it in a cool dark place.
Filter the essential oil using activated charcoal to remove impurities and improve its clarity and shelf life.
Store the mentha x piperita essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of Cinnamomum verum are used to make essential oils for indigestion due to their high content of cinnamaldehyde, which has anti-inflammatory and carminative properties.

Leaves: Leaves of Cinnamomum verum are used to make essential oils for indigestion as they contain compounds like eugenol, which helps to relax the digestive system and alleviate symptoms of indigestion.

Barks: Barks of Cinnamomum verum are used to make essential oils for indigestion due to their content of cinnamaldehyde, which has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues and relieve nausea.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa essential oils helps with indigestion because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcuminoids (specifically, Demethoxycurcumin and Bisdemethoxycurcumin): These compounds have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help protect the digestive tract from oxidative stress and inflammation, thereby reducing indigestion symptoms.
Tumerones and Sesquiterpenes: These terpenoid compounds have carminative properties, which help to reduce gas and alleviate bloating and discomfort associated with indigestion.

remedy_recipe: Harvest curcuma longa rhizomes in the morning when the dew is still present for optimal quality.
Steam distill the harvested rhizomes at 100-110 degrees celsius for 4-5 hours to release essential oil.
Collect the essential oil from the distillation apparatus and transfer it to a clean container.
Filter the collected essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities or residues.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to preserve its quality.

Barks: Cinchona bark is another primary source of essential oils, which contain alkaloids that help reduce inflammation and relieve indigestion symptoms.
Stems: Cinchona stems are also used to produce essential oils, particularly those containing quinine and other alkaloids that contribute to their digestive benefits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cinchona officinalis leaves during the spring season when they are fresh and full of essential oils.
Dry the cinchona officinalis leaves in a warm air circulating oven at 40c for 8 hours.
Steam distill 100g of dried cinchona officinalis leaves with 2 liters of distilled water for 3 hours.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the distilled water through decantation for 30 minutes.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron membrane filter to remove any impurities and contaminants.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cuminum cyminum essential oils helps with indigestion because it has natural carminative properties that aid in relieving bloating and gas. The oil's active compound, cuminaldehyde, stimulates digestion and improves gut health. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the stomach lining, reducing irritation and discomfort. Additionally, cuminum cyminum essential oils has been traditionally used to alleviate nausea and stomach cramps, making it a popular natural remedy for indigestion and digestive issues. Its benefits are attributed to its ability to regulate digestive enzymes and enhance nutrient absorption.
remedy_constituents: Limonene: Helps with indigestion by reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of indigestion such as bloating and cramps.
Cuminaldehyde: Helps with indigestion by reducing inflammation and spasms in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of indigestion such as nausea and vomiting.

Phenolic compounds (e.g., cuminal and cuminic acid): Helps with indigestion by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can protect the digestive system from oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to indigestion.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are the primary part used to extract essential oils from Cuminum cyminum due to their high concentration of carminative and anti-inflammatory compounds.
Leaves: Leaves are another part used for essential oil extraction, as they contain compounds that help in soothing digestive issues and reducing inflammation.
Stems: The stems of Cuminum cyminum are also used to obtain essential oils, which are believed to possess carminative properties that aid in alleviating indigestion and bloating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cuminum cyminum seeds from mature plants in late summer or early fall with a yield of 1-2 pounds per plant.
Steam distill cuminum cyminum seeds with a 1:5 ratio of seeds to distilled water for 3-4 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil in a clean glass container with a volume of 10-20 ml per distillation.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any impurities or sediment in 30 minutes.
Store the cuminum cyminum essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Carum carvi essential oils helps with indigestion because of its carvone content, which has natural carminative properties. It aids in the expulsion of gas from the digestive system, relieving discomfort and bloating associated with indigestion. Additionally, its antispasmodic properties help to calm the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramping and spasms. This combination of properties makes Carum carvi essential oils an effective natural remedy for alleviating symptoms of indigestion and promoting digestive balance. Regular use can help maintain a healthy gut.
remedy_constituents: Carvone: It helps alleviate indigestion by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract and reducing spasms that can lead to discomfort.
Limonene: It helps with indigestion by reducing inflammation and improving digestion, which can help alleviate symptoms such as bloating and nausea.
Bornyl acetate: It helps with indigestion by reducing anxiety and stress, which can contribute to digestive issues and alleviate symptoms such as stomach cramps and indigestion.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of Carum carvi are commonly used to make essential oils for indigestion due to their carvone content, which has anti-inflammatory and carminative properties.
Roots: The roots of Carum carvi are another source of essential oils used for indigestion, as they contain compounds that help alleviate digestive issues.
Leaves: Carum carvi leaves are also used to make essential oils for digestive purposes, thanks to their carvone and limonene content, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of carum carvi seeds when they are ripe and dry them for 7 days to remove excess moisture.
Steam distill the dried seeds at a temperature of 100°c for 3 hours to extract the essential oil.
Collect the essential oil by separating it from the distilled water using a separation funnel.
Filter the extracted oil through a paper filter to remove impurities and debris that may be present.
Store the filtered carum carvi essential oil in a dark glass bottle away from heat and light sources.

remedies.remedy_desc: Anethum graveolens essential oils helps with indigestion because it contains compounds that have natural carminative properties, which aid in reducing gas and bloating. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of indigestion. Additionally, its digestive enzymes promote a healthy gut environment, breaking down food more efficiently and preventing digestive discomfort. This makes Anethum graveolens essential oils a natural and effective remedy for relieving indigestion and promoting overall digestive well-being.
remedy_constituents: Dill Terpinene: This monoterpene helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramps and spasms associated with indigestion.
Linalool: As an anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic agent, linalool helps to soothe the digestive system and alleviate symptoms of indigestion, such as bloating and discomfort.
Anethol: This monoterpene has carminative and antispasmodic properties, which help to relieve gas, bloating, and stomach cramps associated with indigestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of carvone, a compound known for its digestive benefits.
Seeds: The seeds are used because they contain a high amount of dill oil, which helps to alleviate indigestion and other digestive issues.
Fruits: The fruits of the dill plant, also known as dill seeds, are used to make essential oils that aid in digestion and relieve indigestion symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh dill weed leaves and stems from the plant at dawn when the dew is still present.
Steam distill the harvested dill plant material in a still at 100-110 degrees celsius for 3 hours.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the water by decantation or centrifugation.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter or a glass wool pad to remove impurities.
Store the extracted anethum graveolens essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.
